heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.3°C) and outside temperature (down 3.1°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 9%).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 737 m) and light levels (up 13357 lux).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 13464 lux), cloud levels (up 1742 m), feels like temperature (up 2.3°C), and outside temperature (up 3.2°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 736.8m lower than yesterday, 1007.7m lower than the past week, 1742.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 883m to a maximum of 3770m, averaging 1131.0m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 2140m and 2870m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; on the cooler side. Outside; cold temperatures are forecasted. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and near calm conditions. Chance of light rain, occasional overcast conditions, and periods of sunshine are expected. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
